lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day King Lathas.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And to you adventurer. Have you found a way through the Underground Pass yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Well keep at it. Come to me when you have something to report.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day King Lathas.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And to you adventurer. I assume you're here about King Tyras?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am. What's our plan?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Well, as we've previously discussed, my brother is currently gathering strength in the lands west of here. The only known way into those lands is through the Underground Pass.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Unfortunately, the pass is a treacherous place. Some of my best soldiers have fallen trying to find a way though.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: To make matters worse, some crazed group of cultists has recently made their home in the pass. Their leader, some madman named Iban, claims to be the son of Zamorak himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn't sound good. So what do you need from me?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: If my brother is to be stopped, a way through the Underground Pass must be found. This is the task I have for you. Do you think you're up for it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's only one way to find out. Where do I start?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You'll find the entrance to the Underground Pass at the far end of West Ardougne. My main tracker, Koftik, will meet you there.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Together, you are to find a way through the pass. Once you have done so, return to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Remember, the Underground Pass is lethal. Go prepared with food and armour or you won't last long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds too dangerous for me. I'd better prepare more first.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Very well. Return to me when you are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just passing through.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Fair enough. Return to me when you are ready and we will discuss our next steps.

sections.title: Talking to Koftik or attempting to enter the pass

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, you must be Koftik.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: That I am adventurer. King Lathas told me to expect you. I assume he has told you our goal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said we need to find a way through the Underground Pass. Shouldn't be too bad, I've travelled through many a cave in my time.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: These caves are different... There is a darkness in there unlike any other. You can feel it as you wind your way round the stalagmites... an icy chill that penetrates the very fabric of your being...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been in before?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I have. I didn't go far though, nor did I stay for long. People change in these caves...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take my chances.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Okay traveller, I'll catch up with you by the bridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I remember one warrior. His name was Randas. He stood tall and proud like an elven king...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: That same pride made him vulnerable. Randas' worthy desire to be a great and mighty warrior also made him corruptible.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Within the cave, he heard the voice of Zamorak himself...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Zamorak showed him a way to achieve his goals by appealing to that most base and dark nature... One that resides in all of us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to him?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: No one knows...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Anyway, head on in when you're ready. You'll soon come to a bridge, I will catch up with you there.

sections.title: Talking to Koftik outside the pass again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What are you up to?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Nothing exciting, just keeping an eye out.

sections.title: Exiting back out to West Ardougne
Attempting to enter the cave you exit from after getting sucked into the area below the swamp
Climbing back to the beginning after falling through the swamp
Talking to Koftik at the bridge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Koftik.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Hello adventurer. We seem to have a bit of an issue ahead of us. The bridge is up and I can't see another way across.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not know how to get the bridge down?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I'm afraid not. This is the furthest I've ever been. It does look like someone was here before us though, there's the remains of a camp.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I had a look through their things and found some cloth along with the charred remains of some arrows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Charred arrows? They must have been trying to burn something. Or someone!

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: There's plenty more cloth by the fire. I also found the remains of a book...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Probably just litter.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Well... maybe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: It seems to be written by the adventurer Randas. Have a look.

sections.title: Pulling the lever to lower the bridge
Talking to Koftik after lowering the bridge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Koftik.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Hello adventurer. I'll catch up with you further on. Have a look by the fire if you need more cloth.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Join us...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Death is only the beginning...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I will release you...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I'll swallow your soul...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I can set you free...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Blood... Pain... Hate...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: You can't hide...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Give in to your desires...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: The power of the gods could be yours...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Hear me...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Make them all pay...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Kill... Maim... Murder...

sections.title: Talking to Koftik at the grid puzzle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Koftik.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Do you hear them? The voices tell me things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: The path of the righteous man is beset on all sides by the iniquities of the selfish and the tyranny of evil men.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tyranny of the righteous? What?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: So many paths to choose... Here we must all take our own path.

sections.title: Pulling the lever next to the portcullis
Trying to climb down the well before destroying the orbs
Trying to pick up duplicate orbs of light
Using an orb on the well
Destroying the orbs in the furnace
Attempting to use the pipe shortcut

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Eating me... they keep eating me! Eating me from the inside! Please stop them! I can feel them wriggling around inside me right now... please stop them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Blood, blood... never enough blood to go round. When I lift up my arm like this, it all pours back into my body.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: I hope it remembers to go back inside my arm. And don't even ask me about my legs... how much blood are they going to need?

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Blood is important. We must offer it to Zamorak every day as proof of our devotion. I just hope I don't run out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Oh yes, you're a fine one. Nice red cheeks, shiny hair.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Let's see now, some potatoes, some cabbage... Maybe half a clove of garlic...

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Yes I think you'd make a fine soup. You don't mind do you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I do.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: You're welcome to have some with me of course...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Mwaarrr fnnntchh. Gbpp dng sktd delp?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Kjp lar falut: Gbpp dng sktd delp?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I don't under..

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Mwaarrr fnnntchh. Gbpp dng sktd delp? GBPP DNG SKTD DELP!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello...

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Kill the villagers, burn them all... every last one! I want nothing to survive... nothing but the sweet smell of burning flesh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're ill!

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: What's that? You've never smelt it before? Well, let's just say that it's an acquired taste...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Danger, everywhere danger! But not from man nor beast! This is the danger that is inside you.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: Bring it out, nurture it, cherish it. Stroke it like you would stroke a wounded bird... then strangle it before it takes hold of your very being. Don't say I didn't warn you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: What's that... Is that a dagger I see before me? Why should I bear the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

lines.speaker: Slave
lines.text: For what is a man but this quintessence of dust? And so I say goodnight sweet prince. The rest is silence.

sections.title: Talking to Koftik after the stone bridge maze

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: The voices, they've been telling me things...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Koftik, is everything okay? You're getting me worried.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Don't be friend. Anyway, do you know the definition of insanity?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... what?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: The definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over, expecting things to change.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I spent years doing the same thing, day in and day out. I always wanted more but I never got it. Now, I finally see why...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: You see, once I was insane. But now... now I am finally free...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... what?

sections.title: Crawling through the pipe into the unicorn room

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's too heavy to move with just my hands.

sections.title: Passing through the unicorn room tunnel
Speaking to any of the paladins

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Paladin. I wasn't expecting to see anyone else down here.

lines.speaker: Sir Jerro
lines.text: Nor were we traveller. What are you doing in this most unholy place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a safe route through these caverns, under order of King Lathas.

lines.speaker: Sir Jerro
lines.text: Ah, the king tasked us with the same mission. Alas, a little further on lies the great doors of Iban. We've tried everything, but we cannot enter.

lines.speaker: Sir Jerro
lines.text: Anyway, you've done well to get this far traveller. Here eat...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks a lot.

sections.title: Talking to the paladins again after receiving supplies

lines.speaker: Sir Carl
lines.text: Hello again traveller. You should consider leaving this place. Let us handle things here.

sections.title: Throwing the items into the well
Talking to Koftik in Iban's Lair

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Ah, you've arrived. We've been waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Koftik, you're still here? You should leave.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Leave? But this is my home now. Home with my lord. He talks to me, he's my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Koftik, you really should leave these caverns.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Not now, we're all the same down here. There's just you and those dwarves left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dwarves?

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Foolish dwarves, still believing that they can resist. No one resists Iban.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: You could help! Deep down you know you want it! I know you already dealt with those Paladins. Now you just need to kill the dwarves!

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Head south and through the tunnel. You'll find the dwarves at the other end. Go slay them. Slay them for our lord!

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Nice job killing those Paladins. You're much closer to his side now. Perhaps you could kill those dwarves as well. That would really please him.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Head south and through the tunnel. You'll find the dwarves at the other end. Go slay them. Slay them for our lord!

sections.title: Jumping across the gaps in the bridges
Talking to the Disciples of Iban

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: An impostor... Die, scum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: Iban is our father, our guide. Soon he will rule all life.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: Som Molica Aniul Demonte.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: Hail the great one, my lord Iban. I die for you again and again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that possible?

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: Under Iban anything is possible. Death is only the beginning.

lines.speaker: Disciple of Iban
lines.text: Succumb to the might of Iban, the ruler of this world.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Back away! Back away! Wait... you're human!

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Well good luck with that. We came here as miners, completely unaware of the evil that lurked here. There's no way through, not while Iban still rules. He controls the gateway, the only way to the other side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What gateway?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: The Well of Voyage. It acts as a portal through the last section of the caverns. It's the only way into the west. Iban has corrupted it though. Now he uses it to dispose of those who displease him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll just have to kill him then.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Ha! Good luck with that. If we knew how, we'd have slain him already.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Seek out the witch, his guide, his only confidante. Only she knows how to rid us of Iban.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: She lives on the platforms above, we dare not go there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: You're brave to come to this place. Not many survive down here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I can see.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Don't stay too long traveller. Iban's calls will soon penetrate your delicate human mind.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: You'll also become one of his minions... You must go and find the witch Kardia. She holds the secret to Iban's destruction. She lives on the platforms above.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there, you okay?

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Ooooh, my head... I'm fried.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Too much of this home brew my friend. We make it from plant roots, but it blows your head off.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: You don't wanna put it near any naked flames. I've got a barrel full of it here, want some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Here you go... Hic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aaarrgghh!

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Ha ha! I warned you that it's strong stuff. Have a good meal. It may sober you up a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Your losh... Hic!

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Well maybe a good meal? I've got some spare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. Enjoy your brew.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Oh I will.

sections.title: Talking to Kamen again after receiving free food

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there, you okay?

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Ooooh, my head... I'm fried.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Too much of this home brew my friend. We make it from plant roots, but it blows your head off.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: You don't wanna put it near any naked flames. I've got a barrel full of it here, want some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Here you go... Hic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aaarrgghh!

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Ha ha! I warned you that it's strong stuff. Have a good meal. It may sober you up a bit. I could part with some food for a few coins.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: 75 coins pleashe.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: You'll need to make some room in your inventory first.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: 75 coins pleashe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, I haven't enough money...

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Sorry, I can't just give it away to anyone that walks past.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: 75 coins pleashe.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Enjoy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Well come back any time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Your losh... Hic!

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Well maybe a good meal? I could part with some food for a few coins.

lines.speaker: Kamen
lines.text: Hic!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello my good man.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Good day to you outsider. You should be careful down here. We're the only ones that haven't yet turned.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: If you're not careful you'll become one of them too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who? Iban's followers?

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: They're not followers, they're slaves, they're the soulless...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to them?

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: They were normal once, adventurers, treasure hunters. But men are weak, they couldn't ignore the voices.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Now they all seem to think with one conscience... As if they're being controlled by one being...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Iban?

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Maybe... maybe Zamorak himself. Those who try and fight it are locked in cages, until their minds are too weak to resist.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Eventually they all fall to his control...

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Anyway, I have something that might help you. I crafted these to protect myself from the teeth of the soulless, their bite is vicious.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: I haven't seen another pair which can withstand their jaws...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: No problem traveller. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No wonder their breath is so bad!

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: You think this is funny... Eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, just trying to lighten up the conversation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Klank.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Traveller, I hear you plan to destroy Iban?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: I have a gift for you, they may help. I crafted these to protect myself from the teeth of the soulless, their bite is vicious.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: I haven't seen another pair which can withstand their jaws...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Klank.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Good luck traveller, give Iban a slap for me!

sections.title: Attempting to open the door or search the window of the witch's house before talking to Niloof

lines.speaker: Kardia
lines.text: Blast them dwarves! We need to deal with them!

sections.title: Opening the witch's door or searching window

lines.speaker: Kardia
lines.text: Here kitty, kitty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That witch probably won't take kindly to me going in there. Maybe I can distract her...

lines.speaker: Witch's cat
lines.text: Miaoww

lines.speaker: Witch's cat
lines.text: Sssss! Thou art brave to wander so far into the domain of Kardia, mine mistress!

lines.speaker: Witch's cat
lines.text: But soon thou shalt become a soulless servant of Lord Iban, like all who come here.

lines.speaker: Witch's cat
lines.text: Glad shall mine heart be when I see thee prostrate thineself before the altars of Zamorak the Mighty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No milk for you, puss!

sections.title: Opening the witch's door after finding the cat

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: And...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a strange doll, but I've lost it.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Well it's a good job I found it.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I've heard about these dolls. This here be dark magic traveller. They say the witch resurrected Iban from death. She must have used this doll to do it.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: If this doll created Iban, it can also be his end. Find out what process the witch used to revive him and reverse it. Once that's done, cast it into the Well of Voyage. Iban will be destroyed with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the well?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: On the platforms above. Within Iban's foul temple.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I found this old book as well. It seems to detail how Iban was resurrected. It might hold the information you need to destroy him.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I found this old book by the way. It seems to detail how Iban was resurrected. It might hold the information you need to destroy him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Niloof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Niloof, I found the witch's house.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: And...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a strange doll.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Ah, I've heard about these dolls. This here be dark magic traveller. They say the witch resurrected Iban from death. She must have used this doll to do it.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: If this doll created Iban, it can also be his end. Find out what process the witch used to revive him and reverse it. Once that's done, cast it into the Well of Voyage. Iban will be destroyed with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the well?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: On the platforms above. Within Iban's foul temple.

lines.speaker: Half-soulless
lines.text: Kuluf Ali Monopiate!

lines.speaker: Half-soulless
lines.text: Kuluf Ali Monopiate!

sections.title: Searching the cage with the dove

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: The doll? You mean to destroy Iban? No! I am the son of Zamorak! I can't die! And you... You are his slave now! Even if you kill me you will hear his calls forever! You have already fallen!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: You dare to defy me?

sections.title: Talking to Niloof after encountering Iban

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Niloof.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Traveller, thank the stars you're still around! I thought your time had come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've still a few years in me yet.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I found something I think you need traveller...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The doll?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I found it while slaying some of the soulless. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: I found this old book as well. It seems to detail how Iban was resurrected. It might hold the information you need to destroy him.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Good luck to you, you'll need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take care Niloof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Klank.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Hello again adventurer, so you're still around?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any more gauntlets?

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Well..yes, but they're not cheap to make. I'll have to sell you a pair...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: 5,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 5,000, you must be joking!

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: We don't joke down here friend...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then, I'll take a pair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear, I haven't got enough money...

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: Sorry, I can't sell them any cheaper than that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take care Klank.

lines.speaker: Klank
lines.text: You too adventurer...

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: I am the great Iban, I cannot die!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: You will die, frail mortal!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: Fool!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: I'll swallow your soul!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: You belong in the slave pits!

lines.speaker: Iban
lines.text: Begone from my temple!

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Adventurer, where am I? I can't remember a thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We were losing you to Iban's influence...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: What? Of course, the voices... But they've stopped. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Iban's dead, I destroyed him.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: You've done well! Thank you for saving me.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: But what about you, adventurer? Did the voices not affect you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I don't think so...

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: I see. Well we must now inform the king. Follow me, I'll lead you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At last! I've had enough of these caves.

sections.title: Talking to Koftik outside the pass after killing Iban

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for showing me the way out Koftik.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: Always a pleasure. Have you informed the king about Iban?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Koftik
lines.text: This is no time to linger. The King must know that Iban is dead. This is a truly historical moment for Ardougne.

sections.title: Talking to Niloof after killing Iban

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Niloof.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Traveller, thank the stars you're still around! I thought your time had come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've still a few years in me yet. So what are you still doing down here?

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: Well with Iban and his lot gone, we've been left with a great cavern filled with resources. We thought we'd actually do what we came here for and get mining.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. Well you take care of yourselves.

lines.speaker: Niloof
lines.text: And you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day my liege. Any progress with the Well of Voyage?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Ahh... adventurer. The repair work on the Well of Voyage has been completed. We're finally ready to move against my brother.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The adventurer returns, any news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed, the quest is complete. I have defeated Iban and his minions.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Incredible, you are a true warrior. So do we now have a way through to the west?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite. The portal through the last section of the pass has been damaged. It will need repairing.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I see. I will send our best mages in at once. Once they have made the repairs, we will finally be ready to stop King Tyras.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: For now, I thank you for your help adventurer. Your loyalty is an inspiration to all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how long do you think the repairs will take?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: It shouldn't take the mages long at all. Once their work is done we will finally be ready to move against my brother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume you have a plan?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I do indeed. I recently managed to make contact with the elves that inhabit the western lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elves? I've never seen an elf before.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Well that may well change soon. Apparently, my brother has been causing them quite a bit of trouble. As such, they've agreed to help us get rid of him once and for all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I need to do?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You are to head into the Underground Pass and through the Well of Voyage. The mages should have it repaired by the time you arrive.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Once you are through, you will be met by some representatives of Lord Iorwerth.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: With their help, you are to find my brother and kill him. Once that's done, return to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I'm ready for this yet.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: As you wish. Return to me when you are ready and we will begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the deal with your brother again?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother, King Tyras, was always a bit of an explorer. He went on numerous expeditions to the lands west of here. On one of these expeditions, he was captured by the forces of the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The Dark Lord agreed to spare his life, but only on one condition... That he would drink from the Chalice of Eternity. The chalice corrupted him. He joined forces with the Dark Lord, the embodiment of pure evil...

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: As a result, I had a wall built around West Ardougne to act as a barrier between him and the rest of the world. Tyras is king of West Ardougne, sealing off the city was the only way to stop him and the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I knew people would never believe the truth, so I had the plague made up as an excuse for the cordon. I'm not proud of it, but it was the only way to keep people safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But now we're ready to stop him.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Indeed we are. My brother has been gathering strength in the west, preparing to move against us. But now that we have a way through, we can move against him first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. See you later.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Farewell adventurer. Return to me once you are ready to begin.

sections.title: Talking to any of the paladins post-quest

lines.speaker: Sir Carl/Harry/Jerro
lines.text: You again... die Zamorakian scum!
